content.text: Memory MVP lesson for the NoMemoryVerifier component: Situation: ReadTimeRetrievalPlanner can incorrectly return need_memory=false for ambiguous recall questions. Action: run one short model-based verification call before accepting the no-memory plan; do not use hard-coded substring heuristics. Result: we avoid brittle lexical hacks while still catching recall intent. Lesson: for ambiguous no-memory recall decisions, prefer a cheap model-based verifier over substring matching.

Action: run one short model-based verification call before accepting the no-memory plan; do not use hard-coded substring heuristics. structured.fullText:
Understood.

**Lesson recorded (Memory MVP / NoMemoryVerifier):**
For ambiguous recall queries where `ReadTimeRetrievalPlanner` returns `need_memory=false`, run a **single cheap model-based verification call** before accepting the no-memory path, and **avoid hard-coded substring heuristics**.  
This improves recall-intent detection and avoids brittle lexical hacks.
